intro: Artemisia absinthium gum, also known as wormwood gum, is a resinous substance extracted from the stems and leaves of the wormwood plant. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in the production of absinthe, a potent spirit with a long history of use and controversy. Due to its bitter and astringent properties, wormwood gum is also used as a flavoring agent and preservative in various applications, including food, beverages, and traditional remedies.
